---
format:
  revealjs:
    css: style.css
    theme: black
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    preview-links: auto
    keyboard: true
    touch: true
    help: true
    include-in-header: meta-tags.html
    link-external-newwindow: true
    mermaid-format: png
    footer: "Effective Claude Code Usage — Indrajeet Patil"
revealjs-plugins:
  - fontawesome
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
keywords: ["claude-code", "ai-agents", "cli", "anthropic", "software-engineering"]
description-meta: "A comprehensive guide to using Claude Code effectively as a CLI agent. Learn about tools, slash commands, context management, subagents, planning mode, and more."
license: "CC0 1.0 Universal"
pagetitle: "Effective Claude Code Usage"
author-meta: "Indrajeet Patil"
date-meta: "2026-02-18"
lang: "en"
dir: "ltr"
image: "media/social-media-card.png"
image-alt: "Preview image for Effective Claude Code Usage presentation"
canonical-url: "https://indrajeetpatil.github.io/effective-claude-code-usage/"
jupyter: python3
---

## Effective Claude Code Usage {style="margin-top: 1.2em;"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="62%"}

::: {style="color: #38bdf8; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.95em; letter-spacing: 0.04em; margin-bottom: 1em;"}
From CLI to Cognitive Partner
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.72em; color: #8b949e;"}
Indrajeet Patil &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2026
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.65em; color: #8b949e; margin-top: 2em;"}
Source code on [GitHub](https://github.com/IndrajeetPatil/effective-claude-code-usage/)
:::

:::

::: {.column width="38%"}

::: {.terminal-box}
<pre><code><span style="color:#38bdf8;">$</span> claude
<span style="color:#22c55e;">✓</span> Claude Code v1.x

<span style="color:#8b949e;"># Your AI pair programmer</span>
<span style="color:#8b949e;"># in the terminal</span>

<span style="color:#38bdf8;">&gt;</span> How can I help?
</code></pre>
:::

:::

::::

## What you'll learn {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}

- **Agents vs chatbots** — what Claude Code actually is
- **Tools** — what Claude can call and why it matters
- **Slash commands** — session control and automation
- **Context window** — what fills it and how to manage it
- **CLAUDE.md** — persistent project memory
- **Subagents** — parallelising complex work
- **Planning mode** — explore before you edit
- **Prompting for agents** — intent over instructions
- **CLI vs MCP** — token efficiency in integrations

:::

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.card-green}
**Goal**

Turn Claude Code from a smart autocomplete into a capable cognitive partner that handles complex engineering tasks autonomously.
:::

::: {.card-subtle style="margin-top: 0.8em; font-size: 0.85em;"}
**Prerequisite**

You have Claude Code installed and can run `claude` in a terminal.
:::

:::

::::

# What Is Claude Code?

> *"It's not a chatbot in a terminal. It's an agent that reads, writes, searches, and executes — and loops until the task is done."*

## Claude Code is a CLI Agent {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}

::: {.card-subtle style="margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}
**CLI-first** · runs in your terminal, next to your shell, your files, your Git history
:::

::: {.card-subtle style="margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}
**Agentic** · not one-shot Q&A — it loops: perceive → plan → act → observe → repeat
:::

::: {.card-subtle}
**Tool-using** · reads files, writes code, runs bash, searches the web, spawns subagents
:::

::: {.card-blue style="margin-top: 0.8em; font-size: 0.88em;"}
**Key insight** · the model decides *which* tools to use, *in what order*, and *how many times* until the task is done.
:::

:::

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.terminal-box}
<pre><code><span style="color:#38bdf8;">$</span> claude "add unit tests
  for src/auth.ts"

<span style="color:#22c55e;">●</span> Reading src/auth.ts
<span style="color:#22c55e;">●</span> Scanning test patterns
<span style="color:#22c55e;">●</span> Writing tests/auth.test.ts
<span style="color:#22c55e;">●</span> Running: npm test

<span style="color:#22c55e;">✓</span> 12 tests passing
</code></pre>
:::

:::

::::

# Agents vs. Everything Else

> *"The difference between a chatbot and an agent is the difference between giving advice and doing the work."*

## Intelligence Spectrum {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 11
%%| fig-height: 3.2
flowchart LR
    subgraph TAB["Tab Completion"]
        T1["Predict\nnext token"]
        T2["No context\nunderstanding"]
    end
    subgraph CHAT["Chatbot"]
        C1["Understand\nquestion"]
        C2["Generate\nresponse"]
    end
    subgraph AGENT["Agent — Claude Code"]
        A1["Perceive\nenvironment"]
        A2["Use\ntools"]
        A3["Observe\n& loop"]
    end

    TAB --> CHAT --> AGENT

    style TAB  fill:#1c2128,color:#8b949e
    style CHAT fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style AGENT fill:#0f2d1a,color:#86efac
    style T1 fill:#21262d,color:#8b949e
    style T2 fill:#21262d,color:#8b949e
    style C1 fill:#0f3550,color:#7dd3fc
    style C2 fill:#0f3550,color:#7dd3fc
    style A1 fill:#14532d,color:#86efac
    style A2 fill:#14532d,color:#86efac
    style A3 fill:#14532d,color:#86efac
```

. . .

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card-subtle style="text-align:center; font-size:0.82em; padding:0.5em;"}
Statistic → next token
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card-blue style="text-align:center; font-size:0.82em; padding:0.5em;"}
Understands → responds once
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card-green style="text-align:center; font-size:0.82em; padding:0.5em;"}
Acts → observes → iterates
:::
:::

::::

## ReAct Agent Loop {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 6.5
%%| fig-height: 5.2
flowchart TD
    TASK["New Task"]
    P["Perceive\n(files, tools, history)"]
    T["Think\n(reason about next step)"]
    A["Act\n(call a tool)"]
    O["Observe\n(read tool output)"]
    D{"Task\ncomplete?"}
    R["Return result"]

    TASK --> P --> T --> A --> O --> D
    D -->|No| T
    D -->|Yes| R

    style TASK fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style P    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style T    fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style A    fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style O    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style D    fill:#1c2128,color:#e6edf3
    style R    fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
```

::: {.card-amber style="font-size:0.82em; margin-top:0.5em;"}
Claude Code can run 10–100+ tool calls per turn, reading test output, fixing errors, and re-running until done.
:::

# Tools & Capabilities

> *"A tool is a function the model can call. The model decides when — and how many times."*

## Tool Ecosystem {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 11
%%| fig-height: 3.2
flowchart LR
    ROOT(("Claude\nCode"))

    ROOT --> FILE
    ROOT --> SHELL
    ROOT --> SEARCH
    ROOT --> AGENT
    ROOT --> MCP

    FILE["File I/O\nRead · Write · Edit · Glob"]
    SHELL["Shell\nBash · Commands"]
    SEARCH["Search\nGrep · WebSearch · WebFetch"]
    AGENT["Agents\nTask · Subagents · NotebookEdit"]
    MCP["MCP\nGitHub · Jira · Postgres · Custom"]

    style ROOT   fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
    style FILE   fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style SHELL  fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style SEARCH fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style AGENT  fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style MCP    fill:#3a1515,color:#fca5a5
```

## Tool Categories {.smaller}

| Category | Tools | Enables |
|----------|-------|---------|
| **File I/O** | `Read`, `Write`, `Edit` | Read any file; create or surgically modify code |
| **Pattern Search** | `Glob`, `Grep` | Find files by name; search content by regex |
| **Shell** | `Bash` | Run tests, git, npm, docker — any terminal command |
| **Web** | `WebFetch`, `WebSearch` | Fetch docs, read changelogs, search answers |
| **Agents** | `Task` | Spawn isolated subagents for parallel work |
| **MCP** | Custom servers | GitHub PRs, Jira tickets, Postgres, and more |

. . .

::: {.card-blue style="font-size:0.82em;"}
**Permission model** · dangerous operations (file deletion, git push) require explicit user approval. You control the blast radius.
:::

# Slash Commands

> *"Slash commands are the control panel for your session."*

## Slash Commands Reference {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Session & Context**

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/clear` | Reset conversation history |
| `/compact` | Compress history, keep summary |
| `/memory` | Edit CLAUDE.md files |
| `/status` | Show context window usage |
| `/cost` | Show token cost so far |

**Automation**

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/init` | Generate CLAUDE.md for this repo |
| `/review` | Code review current changes |
| `/pr` | Open a pull request |

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Control & Meta**

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/help` | List all commands |
| `/doctor` | Diagnose config issues |
| `/model` | Switch Claude model |

**Modes**

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/plan` | Enter planning-only mode |
| `/fast` | Toggle Sonnet model |

. . .

::: {.card-amber style="font-size:0.82em; margin-top:0.8em;"}
**`/compact`** is your best tool in long sessions — it summarises history into fewer tokens while preserving all decisions.
:::

:::

::::

# Context Window

> *"The context window is finite. What's in it decides what Claude can reason about."*

## What Fills the Context? {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 10.5
%%| fig-height: 3.0
flowchart LR
    CM["CLAUDE.md\n(persistent memory)"]
    CH["Conversation\nHistory"]
    FC["File Contents\n(read by tools)"]
    TO["Tool Outputs\n(bash, search)"]
    IMG["Images &\nScreenshots"]

    CM  --> CW
    CH  --> CW
    FC  --> CW
    TO  --> CW
    IMG --> CW

    CW{{"Context Window\n~200K tokens"}}

    CW --> MODEL["Model reasons\nover all of this"]

    style CM    fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style CH    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style FC    fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style TO    fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style IMG   fill:#3a1515,color:#fca5a5
    style CW    fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
    style MODEL fill:#1c2128,color:#e6edf3
```

. . .

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.card-amber style="font-size:0.82em;"}
**Fills fast** · large tool outputs (full files, verbose logs) consume tokens rapidly.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.card-green style="font-size:0.82em;"}
**Fix** · use `/compact`, limit reads to relevant files, and trim bash output.
:::
:::

::::

# CLAUDE.md

> *"CLAUDE.md is persistent memory — what Claude should always know, even after `/clear`."*

## How CLAUDE.md Is Loaded {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 8
%%| fig-height: 4.8
flowchart TD
    INVOKE["claude invoked"]

    INVOKE --> G
    INVOKE --> P

    G["~/.claude/CLAUDE.md\n(global)"]
    P["./CLAUDE.md\n(project root)"]
    S["Subdirectory CLAUDE.md\n(if cwd is a subdir)"]
    IMP["@import snippets\n(shared files)"]

    P --> S
    P --> IMP

    G   --> SYS
    P   --> SYS
    S   --> SYS
    IMP --> SYS

    SYS{{"Merged System Prompt\n(injected every turn)"}}

    style INVOKE fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style G      fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style P      fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style S      fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style IMP    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style SYS    fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
```

## CLAUDE.md Anatomy {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

::: {.terminal-box}
<pre><code><span style="color:#38bdf8;"># My Project CLAUDE.md</span>

<span style="color:#a78bfa;">## Stack</span>
<span style="color:#e6edf3;">TypeScript monorepo, Node 22, pnpm</span>

<span style="color:#a78bfa;">## Conventions</span>
<span style="color:#e6edf3;">- ESM only (no require())</span>
<span style="color:#e6edf3;">- Tests: Vitest + @testing-library</span>
<span style="color:#e6edf3;">- Branch: feat/&lt;ticket&gt;-description</span>

<span style="color:#a78bfa;">## Commands</span>
<span style="color:#22c55e;">- Test:  pnpm test</span>
<span style="color:#22c55e;">- Build: pnpm build</span>

<span style="color:#a78bfa;">## Never</span>
<span style="color:#f87171;">- Push directly to main</span>
<span style="color:#f87171;">- Commit .env files</span>
</code></pre>
:::

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

::: {.card-green style="font-size:0.85em;"}
**Auto-generate**

Run `/init` in any repo — Claude inspects the codebase and writes a CLAUDE.md for you. Edit it to add conventions and "never do" rules.
:::

::: {.card-subtle style="font-size:0.82em; margin-top:0.6em;"}
**It compounds** · add a rule once, Claude follows it forever.
:::

:::

::::

# Subagents

> *"Why do one thing sequentially when you can do four in parallel?"*

## Subagent Orchestration {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 10
%%| fig-height: 4.2
flowchart TD
    ORC["Orchestrator\n(main Claude session)"]

    ORC --> T1 & T2 & T3 & T4

    T1["Write\nunit tests"]
    T2["Update\ndocs"]
    T3["Refactor\nauth module"]
    T4["Check\ndependencies"]

    T1 --> A1["Subagent 1\nisolated context"]
    T2 --> A2["Subagent 2\nisolated context"]
    T3 --> A3["Subagent 3\nisolated context"]
    T4 --> A4["Subagent 4\nisolated context"]

    A1 & A2 & A3 & A4 --> MERGE["Merge Results"]

    style ORC   fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style T1    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style T2    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style T3    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style T4    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style A1    fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style A2    fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style A3    fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style A4    fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style MERGE fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
```

## When to Use Subagents {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.card-green}
**Use when**

- Tasks are genuinely independent
- Work can run in parallel (different modules)
- Context isolation is an asset
- Large codebase exploration across multiple dirs
:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.card-amber}
**Avoid when**

- Tasks have sequential dependencies
- Shared files risk merge conflicts
- Task is simple and fast
- You need one coherent decision across all changes
:::

:::

::::

. . .

::: {.card-blue style="font-size:0.82em; margin-top: 0.6em;"}
**How** · use the `Task` tool or ask Claude to "do X and Y in parallel." Each subagent gets an isolated context copy; the orchestrator merges results.
:::

# Planning Mode

> *"Measure twice, cut once."*

## Planning Mode Workflow {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 11
%%| fig-height: 3.0
flowchart LR
    REQ["Request"] --> PLAN
    PLAN["/plan mode\n(read-only)"]
    PLAN --> EXP["Explore\ncodebase"]
    EXP --> DRAFT["Draft\nplan"]
    DRAFT --> REV["Human\nReview"]
    REV -->|Approve| EXEC["Execute\nchanges"]
    REV -->|Refine| DRAFT
    EXEC --> DONE["Done ✓"]

    style REQ   fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style PLAN  fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style EXP   fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style DRAFT fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style REV   fill:#1c2128,color:#e6edf3
    style EXEC  fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
    style DONE  fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
```

::: {.card-green style="font-size:0.82em; margin-top:0.5em;"}
In **planning mode** Claude reads and reasons but **cannot write, edit, or delete files**. Validate the plan before any change lands.
:::

## When Planning Mode Pays Off {.smaller}

| Situation | Without Planning | With Planning |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|
| Refactor across 20 files | Broken imports, partial changes | Coherent strategy, clean execution |
| Add auth to existing app | Picks wrong pattern for codebase | Existing patterns understood first |
| Multi-service change | Inconsistent API contracts | All surfaces identified upfront |
| Mysterious bug | Treats symptoms, not cause | Root-cause analysis before editing |

. . .

::: {.card-amber style="font-size:0.82em;"}
**Rule of thumb** · if the task touches >3 files or crosses service boundaries, plan first. ~5 minutes of planning saves 1–2 hours of rework.
:::

# Trust the Model

> *"Claude Opus 4.6 is a reasoning engine. Give it intent, not step-by-step instructions."*

## Let the Model Orchestrate {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.card-amber}
**Micromanaging**

*"First read auth.ts. Then check the test file. Then look for mocks. Then write a test for login using the existing mock pattern. Don't use jest.fn() directly."*

Over-constrained — model can't adapt when file structure differs from your assumption.
:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.card-green}
**Intent-driven**

*"Add comprehensive unit tests for the auth module. Follow existing patterns. Cover edge cases and error paths."*

Model explores, adapts, and makes better decisions with full context.
:::

:::

::::

. . .

::: {.card-blue style="font-size:0.82em; margin-top:0.6em;"}
**Mental shift** · you are the product manager, Claude is the senior engineer. Tell it *what* and *why* — not step-by-step *how*.
:::

## Prompting for Agents {.smaller}

| Principle | Chatbot Prompt | Agent Prompt |
|-----------|---------------|--------------|
| **Scope** | Single question | Full task with acceptance criteria |
| **Context** | Self-contained | "Follow existing patterns in the codebase" |
| **Error handling** | Re-prompt manually | "If tests fail, diagnose and fix before returning" |
| **Verification** | Read the reply | "Run tests and show me the output" |

. . .

::: {.card-green style="font-size:0.82em;"}
**End with a definition of done** · *"Task complete when: (1) tests pass, (2) no lint errors, (3) PR description written."* Prevents premature stopping.
:::

# CLI over MCPs

> *"Not all integrations are equal. CLI calls are often 20× cheaper than MCP protocol overhead."*

## CLI vs MCP Token Cost {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 11
%%| fig-height: 3.5
flowchart LR
    TASK["Same Task:\nList open PRs"] --> CLI & MCP

    subgraph CLI["CLI Path  ✓"]
        direction TB
        C1["gh pr list --json number,title"]
        C2["~100 tokens\nclean JSON output"]
        C1 --> C2
    end

    subgraph MCP["MCP Path  ✗"]
        direction TB
        M1["GitHub MCP server call"]
        M2["Protocol envelope\n+ schema + metadata"]
        M3["Full API response\n~2,000+ tokens"]
        M1 --> M2 --> M3
    end

    CLI --> RESULT["Same result\nfor Claude"]
    MCP --> RESULT

    style TASK   fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style CLI    fill:#0f2d1a,color:#86efac
    style MCP    fill:#3a1515,color:#fca5a5
    style C1     fill:#14532d,color:#86efac
    style C2     fill:#14532d,color:#86efac
    style M1     fill:#450a0a,color:#fca5a5
    style M2     fill:#450a0a,color:#fca5a5
    style M3     fill:#450a0a,color:#fca5a5
    style RESULT fill:#1c2128,color:#e6edf3
```

## CLI vs MCP Decision Guide {.smaller}

| Situation | Prefer CLI | Prefer MCP |
|-----------|-----------|------------|
| **GitHub PRs / issues** | `gh pr list` — minimal tokens | Real-time webhooks needed |
| **Database queries** | `psql -c "SELECT..."` — direct | Connection pool / auth managed |
| **Jira / Linear** | `curl` + API key — simple | OAuth, token refresh required |
| **File operations** | Native `Read`/`Write`/`Bash` | Almost never needed |
| **Complex auth flows** | When API is simple | SSO, multi-step OAuth |

. . .

::: {.card-green style="font-size:0.82em;"}
**Default rule** · if a CLI tool (`gh`, `psql`, `curl`, `kubectl`) can do the job, use `Bash`. Reach for MCP only when persistent auth state, rate-limit handling, or real-time events are required.
:::

# Summary

> *"Claude Code is most powerful when you treat it as a partner, not a search engine."*

## Effective Usage: Mental Model {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%| fig-width: 8
%%| fig-height: 5.2
flowchart TD
    START["New Task"] --> CHECK

    CHECK{"CLAUDE.md\nup to date?"}
    CHECK -->|No| UPDATE["Update / run /init"]
    CHECK -->|Yes| SIZE

    UPDATE --> SIZE
    SIZE{"Task\ncomplexity?"}

    SIZE -->|Simple| DIRECT["Direct prompt\n+ clear outcome"]
    SIZE -->|Medium| PLANM["/plan first\nthen execute"]
    SIZE -->|Large| SUB["Subagents\nvia Task tool"]

    DIRECT & PLANM & SUB --> CTX

    CTX{"Context\nfull?"}
    CTX -->|Yes| COMPACT["/compact"]
    CTX -->|No| DONE["Done ✓"]
    COMPACT --> DONE

    style START   fill:#2d1b69,color:#c4b5fd
    style CHECK   fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style UPDATE  fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style SIZE    fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style DIRECT  fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style PLANM   fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style SUB     fill:#1e3a2f,color:#86efac
    style CTX     fill:#0c2a3a,color:#7dd3fc
    style COMPACT fill:#3a2400,color:#fcd34d
    style DONE    fill:#14532d,color:#bbf7d0
```

::: {.card-green style="font-size:0.75em;"}
CLAUDE.md for memory · `/plan` for complex tasks · Subagents for parallel work · `/compact` when context fills · CLI > MCP for token efficiency · Intent over instructions
:::

## Thank You

<br>

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 0.82em; color: #8b949e; margin-bottom: 1.2em;"}
Other [slide decks](https://sites.google.com/site/indrajeetspatilmorality/presentations) on software engineering practices
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.1em;"}

[{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://www.linkedin.com/in/indrajeet-patil-ph-d-397865174/)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
[{{< fa brands github >}}](http://github.com/IndrajeetPatil)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
[{{< fa solid envelope >}}](mailto:patilindrajeet.science@gmail.com)

:::

<br>

::: {.terminal-box style="max-width: 400px; margin: 0 auto;"}
<pre><code><span style="color:#38bdf8;">$</span> claude --help
<span style="color:#22c55e;">Claude Code</span> — AI pair programmer
<span style="color:#8b949e;">Use it well. Ship great software.</span>
</code></pre>
:::
